metadata.purpose: EigoMaster Demo Data - Realistic Sample for Presentations
metadata.description: Complete sample dataset including 50 students, 5 teachers, 30 listening questions, 100 vocabulary words, 20 writing prompts, 150+ attempts, and learning progress data

sample_classes.name: 基礎クラス
sample_classes.focus: Fundamental listening and vocabulary for EIKEN 2-3 level

sample_classes.name: 標準クラス
sample_classes.focus: EIKEN Pre-1 preparation with mixed difficulty

sample_listening_questions.title: At the Airport - Gate Information
sample_listening_questions.script: Attention all passengers on Flight 202 to Tokyo. Gate change announcement: Flight 202 will now depart from Gate 15 instead of Gate 12...
choices: The flight is departing from Gate 12
The flight is departing from Gate 15
The flight is boarding at Gate 12
sample_listening_questions.explanation: The announcer clearly states the flight will depart from Gate 15 instead of Gate 12.

sample_listening_questions.title: Environmental Science Lecture Excerpt
sample_listening_questions.script: According to recent studies, global temperatures have risen approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius since pre-industrial times...
choices: Temperatures have risen 2.1 degrees since pre-industrial times
Temperature rise is approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius
Predicted rise is 1 to 2 degrees by 2100
sample_listening_questions.explanation: The speaker explicitly states approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius rise since pre-industrial times.

sample_listening_questions.title: Quantum Computing and Cryptography
sample_listening_questions.script: Quantum computing presents both unprecedented opportunities and existential threats to current cryptographic systems...
choices: Quantum computers are slower than classical computers
RSA encryption is unaffected by quantum computing
Breaking current encryption could occur in 10-15 years
sample_listening_questions.explanation: The key timeline mentioned is 10 to 15 years for quantum computers powerful enough to break current encryption.

sample_vocabulary.example_sentence: His ambition is to become a successful entrepreneur.

sample_vocabulary.meaning: 広がっている、浸透している
sample_vocabulary.example_sentence: The issue of climate change is pervasive in global discussions.
His magnanimous gesture toward his rival earned him respect.

sample_writing_prompts.prompt: Write about a trip that had a significant impact on your perspective or life.
sample_writing_prompts.example_answer: Last summer, I traveled to rural Japan for two weeks. The experience changed my perspective on life significantly. Living in a small farming village, I learned the value of simplicity and community...

sample_writing_prompts.topic: The Balance Between Individual Freedom and Collective Good
sample_writing_prompts.prompt: Discuss tensions between personal liberty and social responsibility.
sample_writing_prompts.example_answer: Democracies constantly balance individual freedom against collective good. Classic examples emerge during public health crises: vaccination mandates restrict personal choice for community protection...

sample_writing_submission.content: Corporations possess significant responsibility for environmental sustainability given their substantial resources and influence. Many companies now embrace circular economy models, reducing waste and emissions substantially. However, some corporations prioritize short-term profits over environmental health. Effective environmental action requires integrated regulatory frameworks. Consumer pressure increasingly drives corporate environmental commitment. Sustainable corporations demonstrate that environmental responsibility and profitability are compatible. Strategic integration of environmental considerations into core business operations, rather than treating them as peripheral concerns, appears essential for achieving meaningful sustainability.
sample_writing_submission.teacher_feedback: Excellent argument with strong supporting points. Minor grammar issues in last sentence. Sophisticated vocabulary.

sample_learning_progress.notes: Good focus today, understood grammar better

demo_talking_points: 50 active high school students with realistic progression
3 differentiated classes showing clear performance gaps
30 listening questions spanning beginner to expert level
Detailed teacher feedback on 50+ writing submissions
150+ listening attempts showing authentic success patterns
Clear improvement trends correlating with engagement
Realistic teacher workload metrics (grading, question creation)
Data shows system actually measures learning outcomes
